generations.code:
import sys

def main():
    n = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    a = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    
    dp = [set() for _ in range(n)]
    result = set()
    result.add(0)
    
    for i in range(n):
        dp[i].add(a[i])
        result.add(a[i])
        for j in range(i):
            if a[j] < a[i]:
                for x in dp[j]:
                    y = x ^ a[i]
                    dp[i].add(y)
                    result.add(y)
    
    sorted_result = sorted(result)
    print(len(sorted_result))
    print(' '.join(map(str, sorted_result)))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys

def main():
    n = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    a = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
    
    dp = [set() for _ in range(n)]
    result = set()
    result.add(0)
    
    for i in range(n):
        dp[i].add(a[i])
        result.add(a[i])
        for j in range(i):
            if a[j] < a[i]:
                for x in dp[j]:
                    y = x ^ a[i]
                    dp[i].add(y)
                    result.add(y)
    
    sorted_result = sorted(result)
    print(len(sorted_result))
    print(' '.join(map(str, sorted_result)))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

bugs.description: The algorithm iterates over all existing DP states for every array element, leading to O(n * S) time where S can be up to ~256k (all XOR values × possible last numbers). With n up to 1e5 this results in billions of operations and will TLE.
bugs.triggering_input: n=100000 with a_i values covering many distinct numbers (e.g., 0..500 repeated).
